pundit.name: Clint Dempsey
pundit.take: Rafe "The Noisemaker" sittin' pretty on top of the standings is like watchin' your buddy who never watched soccer stumble into a poker game and clean house — pure chaos with zero logic. Yesterday, Germany came through for him against Gayden's England fail-safe, and now he's all tied up with Zach "Mustard Boy." Now, Mustard Boy hopes his Saudi Arabia pick can steal somethin' against Devin's Spain, but let's be real, Zach only chose Saudi Arabia 'cause he thought oil money would translate to goals. Meanwhile, Ghost Pepper's Spain is lookin' solid like your favorite pair of boots — reliable, stylish, and ready to stomp. I reckon Spain's about to make Mustard Boy's dreams turn to mustard-colored nightmares. Expect Spain to paint Atlanta red and teach Saudi a soccer lesson they won't forget.

pundit_takes.headline: Germany victory secures Rafe's spot at top of standings
pundit_takes.subtitle: 15-year-old leads by science project luck more than skill.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Brazil overshadowed by underrated Saudi Arabia fixture
pundit_takes.subtitle: "Sometimes tier one doesn't mean top performance," Zach reportedly sighed.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper's Spain looks to recover against Mustard Boy's underdogs
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin hopes to spice things up with Saudi upset.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G bets big on Egypt as New Zealand showdown looms
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner banking on an Egyptian win to climb the standings ladder.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner, how does it feel to get leapfrogged by a kid who can't draft?
Rafe's draft strategy: pick the non-qualifiers and still end up first.
Rafe and Gayden's 0-point head-to-head barnburner: father-son tea party, not a rivalry.
Devin, the model loves you more than your record does. Ghost Pepper, indeed.
